## Per-event correction when batches mix

Today every record in a given probe drain carries the same
`batch` (the runtime commits to one `inner` per run), so
`correction` is a single constant across the drain. If a
future probe ever mixes `batch` values within one drain —
e.g. an actor whose scopes cover both fast-path singles
(`batch = 1`, framing term paid in full) and slow-path
batches (`batch = 1000`, framing term ≈ 0) — the correct
overhead-adjusted mean per band requires per-record
correction tracked per band, not a single global average.
The bot thinks the change at that point is:

- drain loop accumulates `band_correction_sum[i]` alongside
  `band_sum[i]`,
- `adj_mean[i] = (band_sum[i] - band_correction_sum[i]) /
  band_count[i]`.

Deferred until that situation arises.
input.new_string:
## Per-event correction when batches mix

`TProbe::report` averages the per-event correction across
all drained records and passes a single constant to
`band_table::render`. Every record in a given probe drain
today carries the same `batch` (the runtime commits to one
`inner` per run), so that global average *is* the
per-record correction — exact.

If a future probe ever mixes `batch` values within one
drain — e.g. an actor whose scopes cover both fast-path
singles (`batch = 1`, framing term paid in full) and
slow-path batches (`batch = 1000`, framing term ≈ 0) —
records with small batches carry large corrections and
records with large batches carry tiny ones. If those
records cluster by band (small-batch records in higher-
value bands, say), a global average under-corrects some
bands and over-corrects others. The bot thinks the fix at
that point is:

- drain loop accumulates `band_correction_sum[i]` alongside
  `band_sum[i]`,
- `adj_mean[i] = (band_sum[i] - band_correction_sum[i]) /
  band_count[i]`.

Deferred until that situation arises. The `adj mean`
column's accuracy in the mixed-batch case should be
considered approximate until then.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Overhead model

Definitions and policy for what `tprobe` calls "overhead"
and what gets subtracted from measured values.

## Measured quantities

Calibration runs a two-point fit on an empty `black_box(1)`
bench and produces two numbers, both recorded on the
`Overhead` struct.

### Framing — `framing_ticks` (per scope)

The hardware cost of the two `rdtsc` reads that bracket
every `TProbe` scope. Paid once per scope, independent of
how many events the scope covers. On modern x86 this is
~20 ticks (~5 ns at ~3.7 GHz).

Why two-point (and not single-point)? `rdtsc` is not a
serializing instruction, so two back-to-back reads with
nothing between them can execute out-of-order and measure
as little as 0–1 ticks — well below the true apparatus
cost. Bracketing with `_mm_lfence` drains the pipeline
harder than real work does and overcorrects. The two-point
fit avoids both biases by varying an `inner` size and
solving

```
raw_delta = framing + inner · loop_per_iter
```

in `f64` arithmetic (no integer truncation) across two
empty benches at `N_LOW = 100` and `N_HIGH = 10_000`. The
intercept is framing; the slope is `loop_per_iter` (below).

Noise amplification on the intercept is
`d(framing) / d(raw_low) = N_HIGH / (N_HIGH − N_LOW) ≈ 1.01`
with the current constants — any slop on `raw_low`
propagates roughly 1:1 to framing.

### Loop-per-iter — `loop_per_iter_ticks` (per inner iter)

The slope of the same fit — the per-iteration cost of the
calibration's empty inner loop, one `black_box(1)` per
iteration. On modern x86 this is small, on the order of one
tick per iter.

It models the loop scaffolding a real measurement loop also
pays: loop branch, counter increment, plus whatever cost a
"does-nothing" iteration has. It is not a perfect model of
a real dispatch loop — the real loop doesn't execute the
`black_box(1)` itself, so its per-iter scaffolding is
slightly cheaper than what calibration reports.

## Unmeasurable overhead

Things calibration does not separately measure and that are
not separately subtractable from stored scope deltas:

- Cache misses and branch mispredicts inside the scope.
- Context switches and OS interrupts during the scope.
- Microarchitectural noise (TLB misses, frequency changes,
  thermal throttling).

These are part of what the real system experiences and
remain in the measurement. The bot thinks they typically
manifest in the `p99-max` tail of the histogram — which is
why the band-table report also prints `mean min-p99`
(whole-distribution statistics excluding the top 1 % tail,
robust against rare outliers).

No future calibration can separate these per-event without
a fundamentally different measurement technique; they are
the irreducible noise floor of "whatever happened between
the two `rdtsc` reads". Keeping them in the measurement is
honest — they are part of the real cost the measured code
pays.

## Correction policy

`Overhead::per_event_ticks(batch)` returns the per-event
correction:

```
correction = framing_ticks / batch + loop_per_iter_ticks
                └── amortized       └── paid per inner
                    over the scope      iteration
```

At `batch = 1` the framing term is paid in full; at
`batch = N` it is spread across `N` events and rounds
toward zero as `N` grows. `loop_per_iter_ticks` is added
because the real measurement loop pays per-iter scaffolding
(loop branch + counter increment) that the empty-bench fit
captures. The sum is rounded to the nearest tick.

The bot thinks this matches "cost of the work alone" more
closely than framing-only: the real loop's per-iter
scaffolding is structural overhead relative to the dispatch
work, and `black_box(1)`'s iter cost is a reasonable proxy.
The tradeoff is a small downward bias — the real loop
doesn't execute the `black_box(1)` itself, so the measured
`loop_per_iter_ticks` is slightly larger than the real
loop's scaffolding. The bot thinks the bias is on the order
of a tick per event and is worth the simpler story in the
output.

### Where the correction is applied

`TProbe`'s histogram stores **raw** per-event values
unchanged — no in-place subtraction. The correction is
averaged across drained records and passed to the
band-table renderer, which displays it in a rightmost
`adj mean` column alongside the raw columns. Each row's
(and each mean summary row's) raw mean has the correction
subtracted, clamped at 0, for display; the other columns
(`first` / `last` / `range` / `count` / `mean`) show raw
unchanged.

`stdev` rows leave the `adj mean` column blank: standard
deviation is shift-invariant, so subtracting a constant
from every sample doesn't change it.

The bot thinks raw-and-adjusted side-by-side is more
honest than hiding raw behind a subtracted mean: readers
see the physical per-event cost the probe actually
measured plus a derived "cost of the work alone" column,
and band placement + distribution shape are unaffected by
the correction choice.

## Per-event correction when batches mix

Today every record in a given probe drain carries the same
`batch` (the runtime commits to one `inner` per run), so
`correction` is a single constant across the drain. If a
future probe ever mixes `batch` values within one drain —
e.g. an actor whose scopes cover both fast-path singles
(`batch = 1`, framing term paid in full) and slow-path
batches (`batch = 1000`, framing term ≈ 0) — the correct
overhead-adjusted mean per band requires per-record
correction tracked per band, not a single global average.
The bot thinks the change at that point is:

- drain loop accumulates `band_correction_sum[i]` alongside
  `band_sum[i]`,
- `adj_mean[i] = (band_sum[i] - band_correction_sum[i]) /
  band_count[i]`.

Deferred until that situation arises.

- **No change to histogram semantics.** `TProbe::report`
  still subtracts the per-event correction and clamps at
  1 (the histogram lower bound). Only the correction value
  changes; the subtraction path is untouched.

## Future work: linkme/inventory benchmark harness
input.new_string:
- **No change to histogram semantics.** `TProbe::report`
  still subtracts the per-event correction and clamps at
  1 (the histogram lower bound). Only the correction value
  changes; the subtraction path is untouched.

## Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3)

Changes `TProbe::report` to store **raw** per-event values
in the histogram (no in-place subtraction) and adds a
rightmost `adj mean` column to the band-table report where
the correction is displayed. The existing columns
(`first` / `last` / `range` / `count` / `mean`) now always
show raw hardware-tick deltas divided by batch, untouched
by overhead calibration. `adj mean` shows each row's raw
mean minus the per-event correction (clamped at 0); the
two `stdev` summary rows leave the adj column blank since
standard deviation is shift-invariant.

Splitting raw from adjusted makes the probe's measurement
honest: readers see the physical per-event cost the probe
actually observed, then a derived "cost of the work alone"
value beside it, and can A/B either against other runs.
Band placement and distribution shape are no longer
shifted by the correction choice.

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.3.0-2` → `0.3.0-3`.
- `crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml`: `0.1.1` → `0.1.2`. Behavior
  of `TProbe::report` changes (histogram contents; band
  table layout), so the tprobe crate bumps too.
- `crates/tprobe/src/tprobe.rs`:
  - `TProbe::report` stores `per_event_raw` in the
    histogram without subtracting overhead. If an
    `Overhead` is supplied, the per-event correction is
    averaged across drained records and forwarded to
    `band_table::render`. Docstring updated.
  - Module docstring's divergence bullet revised:
    "histogram stores raw, correction passed through to
    render".
  - Test `report_subtracts_overhead` renamed to
    `report_stores_raw_even_with_overhead` with its
    assertion flipped from 90 → 100 to reflect raw
    storage. The other report tests stay as-is (they
    didn't use overhead).
- `crates/tprobe/src/band_table.rs`:
  `render(kind, name, hist, as_ticks, correction: Option<u64>)`.
  When `correction` is `Some`, the header gains an
  `adj mean` column; band rows show raw + adj mean;
  summary rows `mean` and `mean min-p99` show both; `stdev`
  rows leave the adj cell blank. When `correction` is
  `None`, the old layout (no adj column) is emitted —
  preserves backward behavior if overhead is absent.
  Column widths include the new adj column where relevant.
- `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`:
  Subtraction-policy section renamed to Correction-policy
  and rewritten to describe the raw+adj display pattern.
  A new "Where the correction is applied" subsection
  notes the histogram-stores-raw + band-table-displays-adj
  split and the shift-invariance rationale for blank
  stdev cells. The "Per-event correction when batches
  mix" section updated to describe the global-average
  approach today and flag the mixed-batch approximation.

### Display example (inner=1000, this box)

```
  tprobe: goal1.dispatch [count=20,320]
              first      last     range       count  mean adj mean
    min-p1        0 ns      0 ns      0 ns        0     0 ns     0 ns
    p20-p30       4 ns      4 ns      0 ns    8,828     4 ns     4 ns
    p60-p70       5 ns      5 ns      0 ns    6,794     5 ns     4 ns
    p80-p90       5 ns      5 ns      0 ns    3,467     5 ns     5 ns
    p90-p99       5 ns      7 ns      2 ns    1,048     6 ns     6 ns
    p99-max       7 ns    208 ns    201 ns      183    16 ns    16 ns
    mean                                                5 ns     5 ns
    stdev                                               3 ns
    mean min-p99                                        5 ns     4 ns
    stdev min-p99                                       0 ns
```

(Some bands omitted for brevity; the real output always
renders all 12.) Correction at `inner=1000` is tiny
(framing/1000 + loop_per_iter ≈ 1 ns), so most rows'
`mean` and `adj mean` are identical. At `inner=1` the
correction is much larger (framing paid in full), and
the split is visible: `mean=10 ns, adj mean=4 ns` on
most bands.

### Design decisions recorded here

- **Global-average correction (approach (a), not per-band).**
  At constant batch (today's PoC) the global average
  equals the per-record correction exactly; at mixed
  batches it's approximate. Tracked in
  `overhead-model.md`'s "Per-event correction when batches
  mix" section along with the per-band-sum extension that
  would replace it.
- **`stdev` rows leave adj blank, not zero.** Stdev is
  shift-invariant, so adj-stdev would equal raw-stdev by
  definition — printing a duplicate column is noise.
  Blank cells communicate "not applicable here" better
  than duplicated values. Alignment is preserved by
  padding to the adj column's width with spaces.
- **`correction: Option<u64>` threads through `render`.**
  When overhead is `None` (today's `--raw` flag; removed in
  `0.3.0-4`), no adj column renders — the old layout is
  preserved. Avoids forcing the adj column to exist with
  meaningless zeros in the no-overhead case.
- **`mean_w` now includes summary-row widths.** Previously
  `mean_w` was computed only from band-row `mean` strings;
  summary `mean` / `mean min-p99` values could exceed this
  (especially when a single hot band dominates the
  histogram's mean). Collecting summary strings first,
  then widening `mean_w` to include them, keeps alignment
  consistent. Same for `adj_w` when `show_adj`.

## Future work: linkme/inventory benchmark harness

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- Band-table raw + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3)
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4)
- Lower warmup default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5)
- After 0.3.0: add a `criterion` benchmark mirroring the goal1 /
  goal2 workloads, so `tprobe`'s numbers can be cross-validated
  against an established statistical-sampling harness
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.subject: 0.3.0: close ladder + README update
content.description: Final release commit (no -N suffix). Update top-level README.md to reflect: new output format with adj mean column, no --raw flag, 0.5s warmup default, new per-crate READMEs and notes layout. Move completed 0.3.0-* todos to Done.

content.subject: 0.3.0-3: band-table raw + adj mean column
content.description: TProbe::report stores raw per_event in histogram (no correction applied). band_table::render gains an optional per-event correction parameter; columns first/last/range/count/mean show raw; new rightmost adj mean column shows raw − correction. Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).
content.activeForm: Adding adj mean column to band table

content.subject: 0.3.0-2: extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter
content.description: Change Overhead::per_event_ticks to include both framing/batch and loop_per_iter_ticks. Update apparatus: line to show the split so readers see what was subtracted. Update tests. No flag/UX change yet.

content.subject: 0.3.0-1: notes reorg + per-crate README + overhead-model.md
content.description: Move notes/design.md → crates/actor-x1/notes/design.md. Create crates/actor-x1/notes/ and crates/tprobe/notes/. Add crates/actor-x1/README.md and crates/tprobe/README.md. Write crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md defining framing, loop-per-iter, and the "everything else" category (unmeasurable: cache misses, context switches, interrupts — stays in the number). Update code doc-comments in overhead.rs / tprobe.rs to point at overhead-model.md instead of carrying the long prose inline. Docs-only; no behavior change.

content.subject: 0.3.0-0: plan marker + version bump
content.description: Bump workspace version to 0.3.0-0. Add chores-01.md entry for the ladder. Update todo.md In Progress section.

content.subject: 0.3.0-4: remove --raw flag, always calibrate
content.description: Drop --raw CLI flag from goal1 and goal2. Calibration runs unconditionally (it's ~10ms). Remove the "raw (no overhead subtraction)" branch of the apparatus line. Update README.

content.subject: 0.3.0-5: lower warmup default to 0.5s
content.description: Change --warmup default from 10.0 to 0.5 in both goal1 and goal2. Update README.

content.text:
Pre-commit checklist clean for **0.3.0-3**:

- `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- `cargo test` — 23/23 (renamed `report_subtracts_overhead → report_stores_raw_even_with_overhead`, assertion flipped from 90 → 100 to verify raw storage)
- `cargo install --path crates/actor-x1` — 0.3.0-3 installed
- Retest (earlier output): apparatus line unchanged; band-table now shows new `adj mean` column. At `inner=1000` correction rounds to ~1 ns so `mean` and `adj mean` are nearly identical per row; at `inner=1` correction is ~6 ns and the split is visible (`10 ns → 4 ns`). `stdev` and `stdev min-p99` rows correctly blank in the adj column.

**Summary of 0.3.0-3:**

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.3.0-2 → 0.3.0-3`.
- `crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml`: `0.1.1 → 0.1.2` (behavior of `TProbe::report` + band-table layout change).
- `crates/tprobe/src/tprobe.rs`: `TProbe::report` stores raw `per_event_raw` in the histogram (no in-place subtraction); averages per-event correction across drained records and forwards it to `band_table::render`. Docstring + divergence-bullet updated. Test renamed + assertion flipped.
- `crates/tprobe/src/band_table.rs`: `render` gains `correction: Option<u64>`. When `Some`, renders `adj mean` column for band rows + `mean`/`mean min-p99` summaries; `stdev`/`stdev min-p99` leave the column blank. `mean_w` / `adj_w` widen to accommodate summary strings too (fixes a pre-existing alignment limitation). `None` path preserves the old layout byte-for-byte.
- `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`: "Subtraction policy" → "Correction policy" with a new "Where the correction is applied" subsection describing raw-stored + adj-displayed split. "Per-event correction when batches mix" updated to reflect the current global-average implementation.
- `notes/chores-01.md`: new section with a display example and four design decisions (global-average correction; stdev blank not zero; `Option<u64>` for the none path; `mean_w` summary-inclusive).
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.3.0-2 → Done; 0.3.0-3 → In Progress; reference `[12]` added.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: Working the 0.3.0 ladder on actor-x1; step 0.3.0-3 (band-table raw columns plus new adj mean column) is complete and checks green. Next: you review the diff, then I'll prep the commit title and body for your approval.

lastPrompt: adj mean column needs to move to the right. I'm a little surprised we have 11M 0 in p1-p10 when those should be raw?

content.text:
Two answers.

**On the `0` — it's correct raw data, just display-precision.** Ticks mode confirms it:

```
    p1-p10         1 tk          1 tk          1 tk      183,830      1 tk      0 tk
    p10-p20       37 tk         37 tk          1 tk      183,647     37 tk     15 tk
    p50-p60       38 tk         38 tk          1 tk    1,579,829     38 tk     16 tk
```

p1-p10 holds 183,830 records at **1 tick** (not 0). 1 tick / ~3.78 tk/ns ≈ 0.26 ns, which rounds to "0 ns" with zero-decimal display. These are OoO-coalesced rdtsc pairs — the two `rdtsc` instructions in the scope reorder into one pipeline slot and report ~0 delta. Before 0.3.0-3 they were indistinguishable from "delta ≤ framing → saturating_sub(21) → clamp to 1" — both fed the same 1-tick bucket. Now that we store raw, the 1-tick cluster (OoO artifact) is separated from the real 37–38 tick work cluster; it just happens to land at the same displayed "0 ns" as before because of integer-ns rounding.

Real dispatch cost at inner=1: **~38 tk ≈ 10 ns** (framing ~21 + handle_message ~17). Adjusted: ~16 tk ≈ 4 ns (which matches what `inner=1000` measures directly). Both views agree.

Real story: raw is correct, display precision just hides it at the sub-ns scale. `-t` exposes the truth when it matters.

**On alignment — let me widen the gap before the adj column.** Currently the header `adj mean` sits only 2 chars past `mean`, while the data has 5 chars of separation. Easy fix: double the gap before adj.

content.subject: 0.3.0: close ladder + README update
content.description: Final release commit (no -N suffix). Update top-level README.md to reflect: new output format with adj mean column, no --raw flag, 0.5s warmup default, new per-crate READMEs and notes layout. Move completed 0.3.0-* todos to Done.

content.subject: 0.3.0-3: band-table raw + adj mean column
content.description: TProbe::report stores raw per_event in histogram (no correction applied). band_table::render gains an optional per-event correction parameter; columns first/last/range/count/mean show raw; new rightmost adj mean column shows raw − correction. Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).
content.activeForm: Adding adj mean column to band table

content.subject: 0.3.0-2: extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter
content.description: Change Overhead::per_event_ticks to include both framing/batch and loop_per_iter_ticks. Update apparatus: line to show the split so readers see what was subtracted. Update tests. No flag/UX change yet.

content.subject: 0.3.0-1: notes reorg + per-crate README + overhead-model.md
content.description: Move notes/design.md → crates/actor-x1/notes/design.md. Create crates/actor-x1/notes/ and crates/tprobe/notes/. Add crates/actor-x1/README.md and crates/tprobe/README.md. Write crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md defining framing, loop-per-iter, and the "everything else" category (unmeasurable: cache misses, context switches, interrupts — stays in the number). Update code doc-comments in overhead.rs / tprobe.rs to point at overhead-model.md instead of carrying the long prose inline. Docs-only; no behavior change.

content.subject: 0.3.0-0: plan marker + version bump
content.description: Bump workspace version to 0.3.0-0. Add chores-01.md entry for the ladder. Update todo.md In Progress section.

content.subject: 0.3.0-4: remove --raw flag, always calibrate
content.description: Drop --raw CLI flag from goal1 and goal2. Calibration runs unconditionally (it's ~10ms). Remove the "raw (no overhead subtraction)" branch of the apparatus line. Update README.

content.subject: 0.3.0-5: lower warmup default to 0.5s
content.description: Change --warmup default from 10.0 to 0.5 in both goal1 and goal2. Update README.

toolUseResult.oldString:
- **`mean_w` now includes summary-row widths.** Previously
  `mean_w` was computed only from band-row `mean` strings;
  summary `mean` / `mean min-p99` values could exceed this
  (especially when a single hot band dominates the
  histogram's mean). Collecting summary strings first,
  then widening `mean_w` to include them, keeps alignment
  consistent. Same for `adj_w` when `show_adj`.
toolUseResult.newString:
- **`mean_w` now includes summary-row widths.** Previously
  `mean_w` was computed only from band-row `mean` strings;
  summary `mean` / `mean min-p99` values could exceed this
  (especially when a single hot band dominates the
  histogram's mean). Collecting summary strings first,
  then widening `mean_w` to include them, keeps alignment
  consistent. Same for `adj_w` when `show_adj`.
- **`ADJ_SEP = "        "` (8 spaces) before the adj column.**
  A single `GAP` (4 spaces) between `mean` and `adj mean`
  read cramped in the output — the adj column looked glued
  to the raw columns, especially in the header. Doubling
  the separator makes the raw-vs-derived distinction
  visually obvious without widening every column.

  summary `mean` / `mean min-p99` values could exceed this
  (especially when a single hot band dominates the

  A single `GAP` (4 spaces) between `mean` and `adj mean`

  to the raw columns, especially in the header. Doubling
  the separator makes the raw-vs-derived distinction
  visually obvious without widening every column.

Alignment tweak + chore update done. Re-verified:

- `goal1 --inner 1000`: adj mean column now 8-space separator from mean; `mean 5 ns` / `adj mean 5 ns` per row, `mean 5 ns` / `adj mean 4 ns` on `mean min-p99`.
- `goal1 --inner 1`: hot cluster at `mean 10 ns` / `adj mean 4 ns`; p1-p10 with 175k records at `1 tk` (rendered `0 ns` due to integer rounding — real OoO-coalesced rdtsc artifacts).
- `goal2`: 10-digit-wide indent-and-padding with the adj mean column clearly offset.
- fmt / clippy / 23 tests all clean after the separator change.

Updated the chore section's display example to match the new spacing and added a fifth design-decision bullet documenting the `ADJ_SEP = "        "` choice.

Ready to prep commit title/body when you want.

Nice — `goal1 5 --inner 10` at 647M samples shows adj mean=4 ns on the hot bucket (matching what `--inner 1000` reports after amortization). Correction of 4 tk / 1.05 ns comes out exactly as `(framing/batch) + loop_per_iter = (29/10 + 0.85) ≈ 3.75 → 4`. The raw=5 ns vs adj=4 ns split tracks.

Proposed commit:

**Title**
```
feat: band-table raw + adj mean column (0.3.0-3)
```

**Body**
```
Changes `TProbe::report` to store raw per-event values in
the histogram and adds a rightmost `adj mean` column to the
band-table report where the overhead correction is
displayed. The raw columns (`first` / `last` / `range` /
`count` / `mean`) now always show hardware-tick deltas
divided by batch, untouched by calibration. `adj mean`
shows each row's raw mean minus the per-event correction
(clamped at 0); the two `stdev` summary rows leave the adj
column blank since standard deviation is shift-invariant.
Readers see the physical per-event cost the probe actually
observed plus a derived "cost of the work alone" value
beside it, without the correction shifting band placement
or distribution shape.

- `crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml`: `0.1.1 → 0.1.2` — behavior of
  `TProbe::report` + band-table layout change.
- `crates/tprobe/src/tprobe.rs`: `TProbe::report` stores
  raw `per_event_raw`. If `Overhead` is supplied, per-event
  correction is averaged across drained records and
  forwarded to `band_table::render`. Test
  `report_subtracts_overhead` renamed to
  `report_stores_raw_even_with_overhead`; assertion flipped
  90 → 100.
- `crates/tprobe/src/band_table.rs`: `render` gains
  `correction: Option<u64>`. When `Some`, renders `adj mean`
  column for band rows + `mean`/`mean min-p99` summaries;
  `stdev`/`stdev min-p99` leave the adj cell blank. Column
  widths include summary strings too (pre-existing
  alignment limitation fixed). `ADJ_SEP = "        "`
  (8 spaces) separates the adj column from the raw columns.
- `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`: "Subtraction
  policy" → "Correction policy", rewritten for the
  raw-stored + adj-displayed split with a new
  "Where the correction is applied" subsection. "Per-event
  correction when batches mix" updated to describe the
  global-average implementation.
- `notes/chores-01.md`: new chore section with display
  example and five design decisions.
- `notes/todo.md`: `0.3.0-2` → Done; `0.3.0-3` →
  In Progress; reference `[12]` added.
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?

content.text:
Same display-precision story as the "0 ns" on means, with a minor twist from how range is computed.

**Range formula:** `range = last - first + 1`. That's an *inclusive count of integer tick values* spanned, not a true span. When all the band's samples fall on a single tick (tight distribution — hdrhistogram's 0.1%-relative buckets collapse close values into one), `first == last` and `range = 1 tk`. In ticks mode you see that 1; in ns mode, 1 tk / ~3.78 tk/ns ≈ 0.26 ns, which zero-decimal formatting shows as `0 ns`.

So `range = 0 ns` is communicating "all samples in this band sat on the same tick bucket" — which is exactly the shape the renderer notes elsewhere (`A stdev min-p99 near 0 alongside a couple of dense bands is a strong signal that the underlying distribution is spike-heavy`). The ticks-mode view preserves the signal; the ns-mode view rounds it away.

The `+1` is slightly confusing on its own — e.g. a band holding only the value 5 shows `range = 1` when zero-width would arguably read more naturally. But changing the semantics is its own small decision (different meaning, changes nothing about the stored data) and is out of scope for 0.3.0-3.

Want me to:

(a) Leave it; the ticks view is the source of truth, treat ns rounding as expected precision loss. Push 0.3.0-3 as-is.
(b) Add a todo to revisit range semantics (drop the `+1` so `first==last → range=0`)? Cheap entry, easy to pick up later.
(c) Something else.

content.text:
Adding the todo. On the adj variance:

The `4 tk` vs `3 tk` between the two runs comes from calibration drift. Per-event correction at inner=10 is `framing/10 + loop_per_iter`, so:

- Run 1: `29/10 + 0.85 = 3.75 → rounds to 4`
- Run 2: `21/10 + 0.93 = 3.03 → rounds to 3`

Framing drifted 29 → 21 between the two calibrations — that's real calibration noise. The two-point fit runs `CAL_SAMPLES = 1000` minimum-passes at `N_LOW=100` and `N_HIGH=10_000` in ~10 ms total, and the minimum-pass converges but doesn't eliminate run-to-run variance in the `raw_low` measurement (which propagates roughly 1:1 to framing per the module docs). At inner=10 the correction is already small (3–4 tk), so ±4 tk of framing noise is a 25–33 % swing in the correction — visible in the adj values.

At inner=1 you'd see the same ~8-tick framing drift directly in `adj mean`. At inner=1000 it's ~0.008 tk — invisible after rounding. Goal1's default regime amortizes the noise; goal1 at small inner doesn't.

Worth addressing when we take up 0.3.0-4 — we already plan to make calibration unconditional there, and could also expand the sample count / duration if the user wants tighter framing. Noting it but not expanding -4's scope unilaterally.

content.text:
That's a real memory bug, and it's the biggest limitation in the current `TProbe` design. Let me quantify.

**What happened.** `TProbe` stores a `Record { site_id, start_tsc, end_tsc, batch }` — 32 bytes — on every `end_batch` call, into an unbounded `Vec<Record>`. The records aren't drained until `report()` runs at the end of the measurement. At `goal1 60 --inner 10` with ~111M msg/s × 60s / 10 = **669M records = ~21 GB**.

Your run matches: the first 60-ish seconds fit until the process hit RAM limits, then swap kicked in and dragged throughput down. The report you captured was actually produced — which means the drain (670M hdrhistogram inserts) did eventually complete, just painfully.

**Design rationale (and its limit).** The `tprobe.rs` docstring says explicitly: *"No delta math, histogram ingestion, or tick→ns conversion happens on the hot path."* That's a deliberate trade — keep `end_batch` to just `rdtsc + Vec::push` so the probe doesn't distort what it measures. It works great for short windows (≤5 s at typical rates) and falls over for long ones. iiac-perf upstream has the same shape.

**Options.** None are in scope for 0.3.0-3 — this is its own thing:

(a) **Hard cap + warning.** Bound `records` at e.g. 16M (~512 MB), stop recording past the cap, print "measurement truncated: N scopes dropped". Simplest. Preserves hot-path cost exactly. Only gets partial data on long runs.

(b) **Periodic drain at threshold.** When `records.len()` hits N, drain inline, clear, keep going. Memory bounded. Introduces measurement gaps during drain (a few ms every few hundred ms at the scale that matters) — visible in the `p99-max` tail.

(c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end_batch` computes `per_event = (delta + batch/2)/batch` and calls `hist.record` directly. Memory fully bounded; measurement is continuous. Adds ~15–20 ns to the hot path. Real cost — at the probe's own ~5 ns reported-mean range, this doubles what we're measuring, which is bad for accuracy.

(d) **Skinnier record.** Store just a `u64` per-event tick value (8 bytes). 4× memory reduction. Still unbounded. 60s at inner=10 becomes 5.4 GB instead of 21 GB — closer but not fixed.

(e) **Just document the measurement-window limit in the README.** Note that window × rate / inner should stay under some memory budget. Cheapest; no code change. Users avoid the trap but the trap remains.

My lean: **(a) + a todo for (b) or (c) later**. Hard cap with a clear truncation message stops the OOM right now and costs nothing on the hot path; the right long-term fix (b/c/hybrid) can be evaluated once we've instrumented the drain cost empirically.

Worth adding to todo and getting through the 0.3.0 ladder first, or fit into 0.3.0-3 before push? Also — do you want to proceed with pushing 0.3.0-3 as-is (the memory issue is pre-existing, not a regression), then tackle this?

Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).

toolUseResult.oldString:
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads

toolUseResult.newString:
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4)
- Lower warmup default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5)
- After 0.3.0: add a `criterion` benchmark mirroring the goal1 /
  goal2 workloads, so `tprobe`'s numbers can be cross-validated
  against an established statistical-sampling harness
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]

[2]: chores-01.md#stage-1-complete-010

[7]: chores-01.md#workspace-split-complete-020

content.text:
Makes sense. 260M records × 32 bytes is 8.3 GB minimum; `Vec` doubles to the next power-of-2 when pushing past capacity, so the allocation is ~2× the working set → ~16–17 GB used, plus heap metadata → 18 GB total. The OS either swaps or the run dies.

I'll add the memory fix as a prioritized todo and propose we ship 0.3.0-3 as-is — the OOM is pre-existing, not introduced in 0.3.0-3 (same Vec-per-scope code path has existed since the probe was vendored at 0.1.0-2). Then tackle it right after the 0.3.0 ladder closes, or as its own patch-level step if you'd rather not wait.

input.command:
vc-x1 push main --yes --title "feat: band-table raw + adj mean column (0.3.0-3)" --body "Changes \`TProbe::report\` to store raw per-event values in
the histogram and adds a rightmost \`adj mean\` column to the
band-table report where the overhead correction is
displayed. The raw columns (\`first\` / \`last\` / \`range\` /
\`count\` / \`mean\`) now always show hardware-tick deltas
divided by batch, untouched by calibration. \`adj mean\`
shows each row's raw mean minus the per-event correction
(clamped at 0); the two \`stdev\` summary rows leave the adj
column blank since standard deviation is shift-invariant.
Readers see the physical per-event cost the probe actually
observed plus a derived \"cost of the work alone\" value
beside it, without the correction shifting band placement
or distribution shape.

- \`crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml\`: \`0.1.1 → 0.1.2\` — behavior of
  \`TProbe::report\` + band-table layout change.
- \`crates/tprobe/src/tprobe.rs\`: \`TProbe::report\` stores
  raw \`per_event_raw\`. If \`Overhead\` is supplied, per-event
  correction is averaged across drained records and
  forwarded to \`band_table::render\`. Test
  \`report_subtracts_overhead\` renamed to
  \`report_stores_raw_even_with_overhead\`; assertion flipped
  90 → 100.
- \`crates/tprobe/src/band_table.rs\`: \`render\` gains
  \`correction: Option<u64>\`. When \`Some\`, renders \`adj mean\`
  column for band rows + \`mean\`/\`mean min-p99\` summaries;
  \`stdev\`/\`stdev min-p99\` leave the adj cell blank. Column
  widths include summary strings too (pre-existing
  alignment limitation fixed). \`ADJ_SEP = \"        \"\`
  (8 spaces) separates the adj column from the raw columns.
- \`crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md\`: \"Subtraction
  policy\" → \"Correction policy\", rewritten for the
  raw-stored + adj-displayed split with a new
  \"Where the correction is applied\" subsection. \"Per-event
  correction when batches mix\" updated to describe the
  global-average implementation.
- \`notes/chores-01.md\`: new chore section with display
  example and five design decisions.
- \`notes/todo.md\`: \`0.3.0-2\` → Done; \`0.3.0-3\` →
  In Progress; reference \`[12]\` added."
input.description: Push the 0.3.0-3 band-table raw + adj mean column commit

content.text:
Good convergence: 1s and 20s runs agree on the shape — mean 18–20 tk, adj 14–17 tk, mean min-p99 matching mean (the hot bucket dominates), and stdev min-p99 ≈ 2 tk in both. The ~3 tk drift between the runs' adj values is the calibration variance we talked about earlier (framing=21 vs 29 this time), and that's the quantization bound at inner=10 where correction ≈ 3 tk.

Standing by for direction on the next step.

content.text:
That's real, and it isn't the probe — it's the workload. Goal2 is two mpsc hand-offs + two context switches + cross-core cache bouncing on every ping-pong round, which makes it highly sensitive to OS-level noise. Compare the shape of the two runs:

**20 s, unpinned**
- Hot bands cluster at ~2,550–2,700 tk.
- Mean 2,910 / 2,922 tk. Mean min-p99 2,860 / 2,845 tk.
- `p99-max` goes up to **4.15 M ticks** (≈1 ms) on actor 0.

**1 s, unpinned**
- Hot bands cluster at ~2,430 tk.
- Mean 2,201 / 2,210 tk. Mean min-p99 2,144 / 2,149 tk.
- `p99-max` maxes at 340 k ticks (≈90 µs).

Two things are moving:

1. **Shift in the hot bucket.** The ~250-tick (~65 ns) shift between the 1 s and 20 s hot clusters is dominated by OS thread migration on your unpinned 3900X. The 3900X splits 12 cores across 4 CCX complexes of 3 cores each; whenever the scheduler bounces either actor thread to a different CCX, every ping-pong that follows has to pay a cross-complex cache-coherence round-trip (~500–1500 ns extra). A 1 s run might keep both threads on one CCX by luck; a 20 s run gives the scheduler many chances to move them.

2. **Tail weight.** Even `mean min-p99` is dragged up a bit by near-tail events (p90-p99 values 5,095–5,815 tk, way above the hot cluster). 20 s accumulates a lot more of those than 1 s.

The README already documents this specifically (*"--pin tightens stdev but can raise the mean"*). Ask your goal2 runs to `--pin 0,1` (same-CCX pair on the 3900X) and you should see:
- Reproducible hot bucket across 1 s / 20 s runs.
- `mean min-p99 stdev` drops sharply.
- Mean possibly slightly higher than unpinned-luck (because you've removed best-case intra-CCX placement variance), but *predictably* so.

None of this is a regression from 0.3.0-3 — goal2's unpinned variance has been there since 0.1.0-7. Worth trying `goal2 20 --pin 0,1 -t --warmup 10` and `goal2 1 --pin 0,1 -t --warmup 10` to confirm the shape agrees once migration is removed.

content.text:
Good convergence with `--pin 1,12`. Numbers across the two runs:

|           | 20 s pinned        | 1 s pinned         |
|---        |---                 |---                 |
| mean min-p99 actor 0 | 1,731 tk (adj 1,709) | 1,758 tk (adj 1,736) |
| mean min-p99 actor 1 | 1,804 tk (adj 1,782) | 1,783 tk (adj 1,761) |
| stdev min-p99 actor 0 | 1,012 tk | 1,002 tk |
| stdev min-p99 actor 1 | 877 tk   | 917 tk   |
| throughput | 0.511 M msg/s | 0.502 M msg/s |

Now within ~2 % run-to-run, stdev min-p99 about 1,000 tk both runs, and throughput jumped from 0.320 M msg/s unpinned to 0.511 M — the ~60 % of extra time the unpinned run was paying was cross-CCX cache shuttle. `--pin 1,12` happens to be a smart choice: core 1 is a physical core on CCX 0, core 12 is the SMT sibling of core 0 (also on CCX 0), so both actors sit on the same CCX and the shared-cache path is maximally warm.

`adj mean` is coming out a clean 22 tk below `mean` on every row — that matches the `per-event at inner=1 = 22 tk` apparatus line, exactly as designed.

Standing by for direction on the next step.

content.subject: 0.3.0: close ladder + README update
content.description: Final release commit (no -N suffix). Update top-level README.md to reflect: new output format with adj mean column, no --raw flag, 0.5s warmup default, new per-crate READMEs and notes layout. Move completed 0.3.0-* todos to Done.

content.subject: 0.3.0-3: band-table raw + adj mean column
content.description: TProbe::report stores raw per_event in histogram (no correction applied). band_table::render gains an optional per-event correction parameter; columns first/last/range/count/mean show raw; new rightmost adj mean column shows raw − correction. Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).
content.activeForm: Adding adj mean column to band table

content.subject: 0.3.0-2: extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter
content.description: Change Overhead::per_event_ticks to include both framing/batch and loop_per_iter_ticks. Update apparatus: line to show the split so readers see what was subtracted. Update tests. No flag/UX change yet.

content.subject: 0.3.0-1: notes reorg + per-crate README + overhead-model.md
content.description: Move notes/design.md → crates/actor-x1/notes/design.md. Create crates/actor-x1/notes/ and crates/tprobe/notes/. Add crates/actor-x1/README.md and crates/tprobe/README.md. Write crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md defining framing, loop-per-iter, and the "everything else" category (unmeasurable: cache misses, context switches, interrupts — stays in the number). Update code doc-comments in overhead.rs / tprobe.rs to point at overhead-model.md instead of carrying the long prose inline. Docs-only; no behavior change.

content.subject: 0.3.0-0: plan marker + version bump
content.description: Bump workspace version to 0.3.0-0. Add chores-01.md entry for the ladder. Update todo.md In Progress section.

content.subject: 0.3.0-4: remove --raw flag, always calibrate
content.description: Drop --raw CLI flag from goal1 and goal2. Calibration runs unconditionally (it's ~10ms). Remove the "raw (no overhead subtraction)" branch of the apparatus line. Update README.

content.subject: 0.3.0-5: lower warmup default to 0.5s
content.description: Change --warmup default from 10.0 to 0.5 in both goal1 and goal2. Update README.

input.new_string:
- **`ADJ_SEP = "        "` (8 spaces) before the adj column.**
  A single `GAP` (4 spaces) between `mean` and `adj mean`
  read cramped in the output — the adj column looked glued
  to the raw columns, especially in the header. Doubling
  the separator makes the raw-vs-derived distinction
  visually obvious without widening every column.

## Remove --raw flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4)

Drops the `--raw` CLI flag from both `goal1` and `goal2`.
Calibration is now unconditional (~10 ms wall-clock on
modern x86). Since the band-table report has shown raw
columns since 0.3.0-3, the `--raw` flag's only remaining
effect was to suppress the `adj mean` column and emit the
`apparatus: raw (no overhead subtraction)` diagnostic —
neither of which add value over the normal output.

- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal1.rs`: `raw: bool` field
  removed from `Cli`. The calibration call is now
  unconditional (`perf::calibrate()` instead of
  `if cli.raw { None } else { Some(perf::calibrate()) }`).
  The "apparatus: raw" branch of the diagnostic is gone;
  the remaining branch is unconditional. `overhead` is an
  `Overhead` rather than `Option<Overhead>`; `.report`
  receives `Some(&overhead)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`: same CLI + main-fn
  changes. The `/// Parse CLI, calibrate apparatus (unless
  --raw), ...` docstring loses the `(unless --raw)` qualifier.
- `README.md`: the `--raw` bullet is removed from the Flags
  section. Other references in the "bot's understanding"
  narrative (e.g. "`--raw --inner 1` reads ~9 ns") become
  stale and will be rewritten in `0.3.0` alongside the
  sample-output refresh; leaving them for now to avoid
  scope-creep into -4.

### Design decisions recorded here

- **`Overhead` not `Option<Overhead>` at the call site.**
  Calibration is unconditional, so the CLI-layer wrapper
  is always `Some`. Unwrapping it to `Overhead` at the
  top-level makes the diagnostic block simpler (no more
  `if let Some(ovh)`). `TProbe::report` still accepts
  `Option<&Overhead>` — the wrapper at the probe API is
  left intact so future library consumers retain the
  ability to opt out of overhead display.
- **`band_table::render`'s `None` path kept.** It's
  pub(crate) and only reached when `TProbe::report` is
  called with `overhead = None`; no longer reachable
  from the CLI. Could be deleted as dead code, but the
  probe API still accepts `None` so keeping the renderer
  consistent costs nothing. If we later commit to
  "overhead always present" at the probe layer, we can
  drop both branches in one step.
- **README sample outputs not updated here.** The
  `## Usage`, `## Reading the band table`, and
  `## The bot's understanding` sections still show
  pre-0.3.0 output format (no `adj mean` column) and
  reference `--raw`. All of it gets rewritten at the
  `0.3.0` closing marker; keeping it out of `-4` keeps
  the diff focused on flag removal.

## Future work: linkme/inventory benchmark harness

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4)
- Lower warmup default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5)
- After 0.3.0: add a `criterion` benchmark mirroring the goal1 /
  goal2 workloads, so `tprobe`'s numbers can be cross-validated
  against an established statistical-sampling harness
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]

- After 0.3.0: add a `criterion` benchmark mirroring the goal1 /
  goal2 workloads, so `tprobe`'s numbers can be cross-validated
  against an established statistical-sampling harness
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]

content.text:
Pre-commit checklist clean for **0.3.0-4**:

- `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- `cargo test` — 23/23
- `cargo install --path crates/actor-x1` — `0.3.0-4` replaces `0.3.0-3`
- `goal1 --help` / `goal2 --help`: no `--raw` flag; only `--warmup`, `--inner` (goal1), `-t/--ticks`, `--pin` remain
- `goal1 0.1 --warmup 0 --inner 1000` runs with unconditional calibration; apparatus line + band-table output unchanged shape

**Summary of 0.3.0-4:**

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.3.0-3 → 0.3.0-4`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal1.rs`: drops `raw: bool` CLI field and its docstring. `let overhead = perf::calibrate();` (unconditional). Apparatus-line branch simplified to a single unconditional print. `probe.report(...)` receives `Some(&overhead)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`: same CLI + main-fn changes. Main docstring loses "(unless --raw)".
- `README.md`: `--raw` bullet removed from the Flags section. Deeper narrative sections referencing `--raw` (`The bot's understanding`) deferred to the 0.3.0 closing README refresh.
- `notes/chores-01.md`: new section with three design decisions (`Overhead` not `Option` at call site; `band_table::render`'s `None` path kept; README sample outputs not updated here).
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.3.0-3 → Done; 0.3.0-4 → In Progress; reference `[13]` added.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: Working through the 0.3.0 ladder for actor-x1/tprobe; current step 0.3.0-4 removes the --raw flag and makes calibration unconditional, and is code-complete and checklist-clean. Next action is to approve the commit title and body so I can push it.

message.content: There is a calibrate warmup but we already have a app level warmup that seems unnecessary

content.text:
Good observation. Let me trace the current flow in goal1:

```rust
rt.run_for(warmup, inner);   // app-level warmup (--warmup 10 s by default)
rt.probe_mut().clear();
let overhead = perf::calibrate();  // internally runs CAL_WARMUP = 10_000 iters
```

And inside `calibrate()`:

```rust
for _ in 0..CAL_WARMUP {         // documented purpose: "CPU freq boost ramp"
    let a = ticks::read_ticks();
    black_box(a);
}
let raw_low  = measure_empty_raw(1000, 100);
let raw_high = measure_empty_raw(1000, 10_000);
```

**You're right**: with the explicit app warmup running first (which already exercises `rdtsc` via `probe.start`/`end` plus the full dispatch loop at whatever the target frequency is), `CAL_WARMUP`'s role — "get the CPU into frequency boost" — is already done. The ~10 K iterations of bare `rdtsc + black_box` take ~25 µs on a modern x86 and don't warm the calibration's actual inner loop (`for _ in 0..inner { black_box(1u64); }`), just the `rdtsc` path. Mostly dead weight in this flow.

**Why it was there**: the probe was vendored from `iiac-perf`, where `calibrate()` was intended to be self-sufficient (no caller warmup assumption). The app-warmup wrapper made that defensive warmup redundant.

**Options:**

(a) **Remove it.** Drop the `CAL_WARMUP` const and its loop from `calibrate()`. Simplifies ~6 lines, no runtime impact in the goal1/goal2 flow. Makes calibration formally depend on "caller warmed the CPU first" — document that in the `calibrate()` docstring so library consumers know. Small, related to -4's spirit (simplifying the calibration flow).

(b) **Keep it.** Defensive, ~25 µs cost, minor code noise but preserves self-sufficient behavior for hypothetical library callers who skip warmup.

(c) **Add a todo, ship -4 as planned.** Defer the decision.

I lean (a) — small, local, and the "calibrate requires warmup first" contract is already what goal1/goal2 assume. Your call.

lastPrompt: There is a calibrate warmup but we already have a app level warmup that seems unnecessary

message.content: remove it although if/when we create a benchmark crate we need to have it in there, where should we have it as part of tprobe?

content.subject: 0.3.0: close ladder + README update
content.description: Final release commit (no -N suffix). Update top-level README.md to reflect: new output format with adj mean column, no --raw flag, 0.5s warmup default, new per-crate READMEs and notes layout. Move completed 0.3.0-* todos to Done.

content.subject: 0.3.0-3: band-table raw + adj mean column
content.description: TProbe::report stores raw per_event in histogram (no correction applied). band_table::render gains an optional per-event correction parameter; columns first/last/range/count/mean show raw; new rightmost adj mean column shows raw − correction. Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).
content.activeForm: Adding adj mean column to band table

content.description: Change Overhead::per_event_ticks to include both framing/batch and loop_per_iter_ticks. Update apparatus: line to show the split so readers see what was subtracted. Update tests. No flag/UX change yet.

Write crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md defining framing, loop-per-iter, and the "everything else" category (unmeasurable: cache misses, context switches, interrupts — stays in the number). Update code doc-comments in overhead.rs / tprobe.rs to point at overhead-model.md instead of carrying the long prose inline. Docs-only; no behavior change.

input.new_string:
### Calibration preconditions

`calibrate()` assumes the CPU is already at its boost
frequency with caches / branch-predictors primed — it runs
the two-point fit directly, with no internal warmup of its
own. `goal1` / `goal2` satisfy this by running an app-level
dispatch-loop warmup (`--warmup`) before calling
`calibrate()`; a benchmark harness that calls `calibrate()`
directly should run its own warmup first (a tight loop of
`read_ticks()` + `black_box(...)`, or the actual workload,
for at least a hundred milliseconds). Without warmup, the
fitted `raw_low` / `raw_high` are biased high — CPU
frequency starts at base clock and ramps to boost during
the first measurement.

### Framing — `framing_ticks` (per scope)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Overhead model

Definitions and policy for what `tprobe` calls "overhead"
and what gets subtracted from measured values.

## Measured quantities

Calibration runs a two-point fit on an empty `black_box(1)`
bench and produces two numbers, both recorded on the
`Overhead` struct.

### Framing — `framing_ticks` (per scope)

The hardware cost of the two `rdtsc` reads that bracket
every `TProbe` scope. Paid once per scope, independent of
how many events the scope covers. On modern x86 this is
~20 ticks (~5 ns at ~3.7 GHz).

Why two-point (and not single-point)? `rdtsc` is not a
serializing instruction, so two back-to-back reads with
nothing between them can execute out-of-order and measure
as little as 0–1 ticks — well below the true apparatus
cost. Bracketing with `_mm_lfence` drains the pipeline
harder than real work does and overcorrects. The two-point
fit avoids both biases by varying an `inner` size and
solving

```
raw_delta = framing + inner · loop_per_iter
```

in `f64` arithmetic (no integer truncation) across two
empty benches at `N_LOW = 100` and `N_HIGH = 10_000`. The
intercept is framing; the slope is `loop_per_iter` (below).

Noise amplification on the intercept is
`d(framing) / d(raw_low) = N_HIGH / (N_HIGH − N_LOW) ≈ 1.01`
with the current constants — any slop on `raw_low`
propagates roughly 1:1 to framing.

### Loop-per-iter — `loop_per_iter_ticks` (per inner iter)

The slope of the same fit — the per-iteration cost of the
calibration's empty inner loop, one `black_box(1)` per
iteration. On modern x86 this is small, on the order of one
tick per iter.

It models the loop scaffolding a real measurement loop also
pays: loop branch, counter increment, plus whatever cost a
"does-nothing" iteration has. It is not a perfect model of
a real dispatch loop — the real loop doesn't execute the
`black_box(1)` itself, so its per-iter scaffolding is
slightly cheaper than what calibration reports.

## Unmeasurable overhead

Things calibration does not separately measure and that are
not separately subtractable from stored scope deltas:

- Cache misses and branch mispredicts inside the scope.
- Context switches and OS interrupts during the scope.
- Microarchitectural noise (TLB misses, frequency changes,
  thermal throttling).

These are part of what the real system experiences and
remain in the measurement. The bot thinks they typically
manifest in the `p99-max` tail of the histogram — which is
why the band-table report also prints `mean min-p99`
(whole-distribution statistics excluding the top 1 % tail,
robust against rare outliers).

No future calibration can separate these per-event without
a fundamentally different measurement technique; they are
the irreducible noise floor of "whatever happened between
the two `rdtsc` reads". Keeping them in the measurement is
honest — they are part of the real cost the measured code
pays.

## Correction policy

`Overhead::per_event_ticks(batch)` returns the per-event
correction:

```
correction = framing_ticks / batch + loop_per_iter_ticks
                └── amortized       └── paid per inner
                    over the scope      iteration
```

At `batch = 1` the framing term is paid in full; at
`batch = N` it is spread across `N` events and rounds
toward zero as `N` grows. `loop_per_iter_ticks` is added
because the real measurement loop pays per-iter scaffolding
(loop branch + counter increment) that the empty-bench fit
captures. The sum is rounded to the nearest tick.

The bot thinks this matches "cost of the work alone" more
closely than framing-only: the real loop's per-iter
scaffolding is structural overhead relative to the dispatch
work, and `black_box(1)`'s iter cost is a reasonable proxy.
The tradeoff is a small downward bias — the real loop
doesn't execute the `black_box(1)` itself, so the measured
`loop_per_iter_ticks` is slightly larger than the real
loop's scaffolding. The bot thinks the bias is on the order
of a tick per event and is worth the simpler story in the
output.

### Where the correction is applied

`TProbe`'s histogram stores **raw** per-event values
unchanged — no in-place subtraction. The correction is
averaged across drained records and passed to the
band-table renderer, which displays it in a rightmost
`adj mean` column alongside the raw columns. Each row's
(and each mean summary row's) raw mean has the correction
subtracted, clamped at 0, for display; the other columns
(`first` / `last` / `range` / `count` / `mean`) show raw
unchanged.

`stdev` rows leave the `adj mean` column blank: standard
deviation is shift-invariant, so subtracting a constant
from every sample doesn't change it.

The bot thinks raw-and-adjusted side-by-side is more
honest than hiding raw behind a subtracted mean: readers
see the physical per-event cost the probe actually
measured plus a derived "cost of the work alone" column,
and band placement + distribution shape are unaffected by
the correction choice.

## Per-event correction when batches mix

`TProbe::report` averages the per-event correction across
all drained records and passes a single constant to
`band_table::render`. Every record in a given probe drain
today carries the same `batch` (the runtime commits to one
`inner` per run), so that global average *is* the
per-record correction — exact.

If a future probe ever mixes `batch` values within one
drain — e.g. an actor whose scopes cover both fast-path
singles (`batch = 1`, framing term paid in full) and
slow-path batches (`batch = 1000`, framing term ≈ 0) —
records with small batches carry large corrections and
records with large batches carry tiny ones. If those
records cluster by band (small-batch records in higher-
value bands, say), a global average under-corrects some
bands and over-corrects others. The bot thinks the fix at
that point is:

- drain loop accumulates `band_correction_sum[i]` alongside
  `band_sum[i]`,
- `adj_mean[i] = (band_sum[i] - band_correction_sum[i]) /
  band_count[i]`.

Deferred until that situation arises. The `adj mean`
column's accuracy in the mixed-batch case should be
considered approximate until then.

  unconditional (`perf::calibrate()` instead of

  receives `Some(&overhead)`.

  sample-output refresh; leaving them for now to avoid

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- Lower warmup default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5)
- After 0.3.0: add a `criterion` benchmark mirroring the goal1 /
  goal2 workloads, so `tprobe`'s numbers can be cross-validated
  against an established statistical-sampling harness
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]

content.subject: 0.3.0: close ladder + README update
content.description: Final release commit (no -N suffix). Update top-level README.md to reflect: new output format with adj mean column, no --raw flag, 0.5s warmup default, new per-crate READMEs and notes layout. Move completed 0.3.0-* todos to Done.

content.subject: 0.3.0-3: band-table raw + adj mean column
content.description: TProbe::report stores raw per_event in histogram (no correction applied). band_table::render gains an optional per-event correction parameter; columns first/last/range/count/mean show raw; new rightmost adj mean column shows raw − correction. Summary rows: mean and mean min-p99 get adj values in the new column; stdev rows leave it blank (shift-invariant). Use global avg correction (approach a — exact for today's constant-batch PoC).
content.activeForm: Adding adj mean column to band table

content.subject: 0.3.0-2: extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter
content.description: Change Overhead::per_event_ticks to include both framing/batch and loop_per_iter_ticks. Update apparatus: line to show the split so readers see what was subtracted. Update tests. No flag/UX change yet.

content.subject: 0.3.0-1: notes reorg + per-crate README + overhead-model.md
content.description: Move notes/design.md → crates/actor-x1/notes/design.md. Create crates/actor-x1/notes/ and crates/tprobe/notes/. Add crates/actor-x1/README.md and crates/tprobe/README.md. Write crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md defining framing, loop-per-iter, and the "everything else" category (unmeasurable: cache misses, context switches, interrupts — stays in the number). Update code doc-comments in overhead.rs / tprobe.rs to point at overhead-model.md instead of carrying the long prose inline. Docs-only; no behavior change.

content.subject: 0.3.0-0: plan marker + version bump
content.description: Bump workspace version to 0.3.0-0. Add chores-01.md entry for the ladder. Update todo.md In Progress section.

content.subject: 0.3.0-4: remove --raw flag, always calibrate
content.description: Drop --raw CLI flag from goal1 and goal2. Calibration runs unconditionally (it's ~10ms). Remove the "raw (no overhead subtraction)" branch of the apparatus line. Update README.

content.subject: 0.3.0-5: lower warmup default to 0.5s
content.description: Change --warmup default from 10.0 to 0.5 in both goal1 and goal2. Update README.

content.text:
Pre-commit clean: fmt, clippy, 23/23 tests, install verified. Behavior unchanged.

**Updated 0.3.0-4 now includes the CAL_WARMUP removal too:**

- `crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml`: `0.1.2 → 0.1.3` (drops a `pub const`)
- `crates/tprobe/src/overhead.rs`: `CAL_WARMUP` const + its 10K-iter loop deleted from `calibrate()`. Docstring gains "assumes CPU is already warm" note pointing at `overhead-model.md`.
- `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`: new **Calibration preconditions** subsection documenting the caller-warmup contract — how goal1/goal2 satisfy it, and what a future benchmark harness should do (tight `read_ticks + black_box` loop for ~100 ms, or run the workload).
- `notes/chores-01.md`: the `0.3.0-4` chore section now lists the CAL_WARMUP-related changes and adds two more design-decision bullets (why fold this into -4, where the future benchmark-crate warmup helper belongs).
- `notes/todo.md`: new item specifically for the benchmark-crate warmup helper — proposed home `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or `cpu_boost(Duration)`.

Proposed commit title and body:

**Title**
```
chore: remove --raw flag + CAL_WARMUP (0.3.0-4)
```

**Body**
```
Drops the `--raw` CLI flag from `goal1` / `goal2` —
calibration is now unconditional (~10 ms wall-clock, always
on). The flag's only remaining effect was to suppress the
`adj mean` column that landed in 0.3.0-3; the `apparatus: raw`
diagnostic branch is gone with it. Also removes the
`CAL_WARMUP = 10_000` iteration loop inside `calibrate()`:
goal1/goal2 already run a full dispatch-loop warmup before
calling `calibrate`, making the bare-`rdtsc` warmup redundant
(and it only primed the `rdtsc` path, not the calibration's
actual inner loop). `calibrate` now expects a caller-warmed
CPU and documents that contract.

- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal1.rs`: `raw: bool` removed
  from `Cli`. `let overhead = perf::calibrate()` is
  unconditional. `apparatus:` branch simplified to a single
  print; `report` receives `Some(&overhead)`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`: same CLI + main
  changes. Main docstring drops "(unless --raw)".
- `crates/tprobe/Cargo.toml`: `0.1.2 → 0.1.3` — removes a
  `pub const`.
- `crates/tprobe/src/overhead.rs`: `CAL_WARMUP` const +
  warmup loop removed from `calibrate()`. Docstring gains
  a "caller-warm CPU expected" note.
- `crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md`: new
  "Calibration preconditions" subsection.
- `README.md`: `--raw` bullet removed from the Flags
  section. Deeper narrative references to `--raw` deferred
  to the 0.3.0 closing README refresh.
- `notes/chores-01.md`: new chore section with five design
  decisions including why CAL_WARMUP folds in here and
  where a future benchmark-crate warmup helper belongs.
- `notes/todo.md`: `0.3.0-3` → Done; `0.3.0-4` →
  In Progress; new item tracking the tprobe-warmup helper
  for a future benchmark harness; reference `[13]` added.
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?

- \`crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal1.rs\`: \`raw: bool\` removed

- \`crates/tprobe/src/overhead.rs\`: \`CAL_WARMUP\` const +

- \`crates/tprobe/notes/overhead-model.md\`: new
